title: Tala Jam Pot Covers, 1lb Waxed Circles Bands & Labels for Cover Jam Jars, A full pack for sealing Freshly made Jam, Marmalade and Preserves
availability_text: This item cannot be dispatched to your selected delivery location. Please choose a different delivery location.
features: COMPLETE SET: This Tala pack includes everything you need to seal your homemade preserves: Each pack contains approx: 24 transparent covers, 24 waxed discs, 24 rubber bands, 24 self-adhesive labels all for 1Lb Jars
IDEAL FOR HOMEMADE PRESERVES: Designed specifically for sealing freshly made jam, marmalade, chutney, and other preserves, helping to lock in freshness and flavour
TRADITIONAL AND PRACTICAL: The waxed circles provide a traditional method of preserving, creating a barrier to prevent mould, while the rubber bands ensure a secure fit
CUSTOMISABLE LABELS INCLUDED: Each set includes writable labels so you can personalise your jars with dates, names, or flavours, perfect for gifting or organising your pantry
QUALITY YOU CAN TRUST: Tala is a well-established brand known for its reliable kitchenware, ensuring you get high-quality, durable products for all your preserving needs
description: From the manufacturer Tala Jam Pot Covers A perfect way to add the final touch to your home made product. Proudly made in England by a small team of specialists it is an essential kit for sealing and labelling homemade preserves. Pot the jam / preserve and cover with waxed disc while the preserve is still piping hot. When jam is fairly cold this should be topped with damped cellophane disc and secured with elastic band. The cellophane shrinks when dry to create a tight seal over the jar. About us THE HISTORY OF TALA Tala is one of the oldest and loved kitchenware brands. A trusted supplier, of baking and cake decorating equipment since 1899, Tala has grown to supply cooks and bakers all over the world. Taylor Law established in 1899, by Frederick Taylor and Thomas Law, to satisfy the developing Victorian kitchenware and hardware market across Britain. They produced a wide range of metal goods for the kitchen and garden which were successful and the workforce grew. The factory created products that ranged from baking pans and icing syringes to make delicious homemade food, to galvanised tubs and travellers trunks. The design and production methods remain largely unchanged, involving over 20 hand processes, and most importantly each cook’s measure is made by one of a small group of highly skilled people; be it Graham, Maureen or one of their team.
